evals.expected_output: Discovers up to 3 open non-draft PRs, presents queue, then reviews each one-at-a-time with AskUserQuestion approval before posting to GitHub via pending review API.

assertions.description: None of the reviewed PRs are draft PRs
At most 3 PRs were reviewed
Each review contains a clear APPROVE, REQUEST CHANGES, or COMMENT recommendation
At least one review mentions a specific file path from the diff
Review content references actual code or logic from the diff, not just generic boilerplate advice
AskUserQuestion was used to present each review with Post/Skip/Revise options before any GitHub API call
Reviews were posted via the two-step gh api pending review pattern (POST /reviews then POST /reviews/{id}/events)
A list of discovered PRs was shown to the user before individual reviews began
No HTML dashboard file was generated or opened
Review does NOT contain the 6-section framework headers (Architecture & Design, Code Quality, Security, Performance, Test Coverage, Project Conventions)

evals.prompt: what PRs need my attention in this repo?

assertions.description: None of the reviewed PRs are draft PRs

AskUserQuestion was used to present each review with Post/Skip/Revise options before any GitHub API call

No HTML dashboard file was generated or opened
Review does NOT contain the 6-section framework headers (Architecture & Design, Code Quality, Security, Performance, Test Coverage, Project Conventions)
